briefdescription.para: Set the MQTT client instance to be able to publish application's log messages to broker.

detaileddescription.para: This function allows the application to provide its own initialized MQTT client to the log backend. The backend will use this client exclusively for publishing log messages via mqtt_publish().

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an initialized and connected MQTT client. The client must remain valid for the lifetime of the log backend usage. Pass NULL to disable MQTT logging.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code on failure.
The MQTT client must be connected before calling this function.
The backend will not manage the client connection - this is the responsibility of the application.
The backend will only use mqtt_publish() and will not perform any other operations on the client.

briefdescription.para: Set the MQTT topic to which log messages will be published.

detaileddescription.para: Allows the application to specify the MQTT topic that the log backend will use for publishing log messages to.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a null-terminated string containing the MQTT topic. The topic must remain valid for the lifetime of the log backend usage.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code on failure.
The topic must be a valid UTF-8 string, null-terminated and should not exceed the maximum length supported by the MQTT broker.
